etymology.description: 周(周) (zhōu, "circle; around") conveys the idea of encircling or encompassing an area, while 遊(遊) (yóu, "to travel; to roam") involves movement, often leisurely or exploratory.
etymology.interpretation: The character 周(周) (zhōu, "circle; around") suggests the concept of moving in a circular or encompassing manner, like a journey that covers different places. This leads to the general idea of travelling widely or thoroughly. When paired with 遊(遊) (yóu, "to travel; to roam"), which embodies the act of movement without a fixed destination, it creates the composite sense of 'travel around,' capturing both the coverage aspect and the motion involved.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢周游世界(他喜歡周遊世界)。
examples.english: He likes to travel around the world.

examples.chinese: 他们计划在退休后周游各国(他們計劃在退休後周遊各國)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen jìhuà zài tuìxiū hòu zhōuyóu gèguó.
examples.english: They plan to travel around various countries after retirement.

examples.chinese: 我们用了一整年时间周游亚洲(我們用了一整年時間周遊亞洲)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen yòngle yī zhěng nián shíjiān zhōuyóu Yàzhōu.
examples.english: We spent a whole year traveling around Asia.
